archetypes.name: The Hero’s Journey
archetypes.description: A protagonist departs from the ordinary world, undergoes trials and transformation in a special world, and returns changed. The structure emphasizes initiation, crisis, and reintegration.
examples: Star Wars: Episode IV – A New Hope (1977 film)

archetypes.name: Rags to Riches
archetypes.description: A disadvantaged protagonist rises to success, status, or fulfillment through perseverance, talent, luck, or moral worth.

archetypes.name: The Quest
archetypes.description: A protagonist or group pursues a defined objective, overcoming escalating obstacles that test commitment and cohesion.
examples: The Lord of the Rings (1954 novel)
Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade (1989 film)

archetypes.name: Voyage and Return
archetypes.description: A protagonist enters an unfamiliar world, survives its rules and dangers, and returns home with insight or maturity.
examples: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe (1950 novel)

archetypes.name: Overcoming the Monster
archetypes.description: A protagonist confronts and defeats a powerful antagonist or existential threat that endangers a community or world.

archetypes.name: Rebirth
archetypes.description: A morally or emotionally diminished character undergoes renewal through crisis, restoring integrity or humanity.
examples: Beauty and the Beast (1991 animated film)

archetypes.name: Tragedy
archetypes.description: A protagonist’s flaw or irreversible choice leads to downfall, emphasizing inevitability and moral consequence.

archetypes.name: Comedy (Restoration of Order)
archetypes.description: Social disorder, confusion, or misunderstanding is resolved through reconciliation, restoring harmony.

archetypes.name: Coming of Age
archetypes.description: A young protagonist matures through formative experiences, achieving psychological or moral growth.

archetypes.name: The Revenge
archetypes.description: A protagonist pursues retaliation for injustice, often sacrificing morality or peace in the process.
examples: The Count of Monte Cristo (1844 novel)

archetypes.name: The Escape
archetypes.description: A protagonist trapped by physical, social, or psychological confinement engineers liberation.

archetypes.name: The Sacrifice
archetypes.description: A protagonist relinquishes something of great value for a greater good, affirming moral commitment.
examples: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows – Part 2 (2011 film)

archetypes.name: The Mystery Unveiled
archetypes.description: An investigation reveals hidden truths, progressively reshaping understanding of events and characters.

archetypes.name: The Transformation
archetypes.description: A character undergoes profound internal or external change that redefines identity or worldview.

archetypes.name: The Rise and Fall
archetypes.description: A protagonist ascends to power or prominence and then declines due to flaw, corruption, or shifting circumstance.
examples: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013 film)
